title: columella wrayi
intro: Columella wrayi, also known as the Kalmegah tree or Malabar ironwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Benthaceae. It is native to the Western Ghats of India and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high tannin and alkaloid content, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, providing health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. Columella wrayi can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to promote overall well-being and vitality.
